title: 清洁女工之死
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\209-清洁女工之死-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Hercule Poirot reveals that the murders of Mrs. Upward and Mrs. McKinley were committed by a man motivated by money. He begins by addressing the evidence found at Mrs. Upward's house, including two coffee cups with lipstick traces and the scent of expensive perfume. While these clues initially suggested a woman was involved, Poirot argues they were clumsy attempts to frame one of three women—Mrs. Carpenter, Mrs. Rundle, or Miss Henderson—who had received phone messages. He concludes the feminine traces were planted to mislead investigators, as the killer was actually a man.

Poirot presents a faded photograph of Eva Kane, found in a desk drawer, which has the words "My mother..." written on the back. He explains that Morley Saunders is the daughter of the notorious murderers Craig and Eva Kane, though she was adopted and unaware of her parentage. He asserts that a man, desperate to protect his lineage and ancestral home from the scandal of this connection, committed the murders.

Poirot then identifies the killer as Robin Upward. He reveals that Robin is actually the son of Evelyn Kean (who used the name Evelyn Hope), not Mrs. Upward's biological son. Robin had assumed the name Upward and used a romanticized backstory to gain the affection and wealth of Mrs. Upward. After Mrs. McGinty mentioned the photograph and the connection to the notorious Eva Kane, Robin realized Mrs. Upward, who was superstitious about heredity, would never tolerate a foster son with such a background. To protect his inheritance and conceal his true identity, Robin killed to ensure the secret remained buried.
